atoms-avatar--docs.name: Docs
atoms-avatar--docs.title: Atoms/Avatar
atoms-avatar--docs.content:
import { Meta, Controls } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";
import * as AvatarStories from "./Avatar.stories";

<Meta of={AvatarStories} title="Atoms/Avatar" />

# Avatar

A small circular image component representing an author or user. Supports linking, fallback behavior when the image fails to load, and accessible labeling.

## Usage

```tsx
import { Avatar } from 'chop-logic-components';
import { LinkTarget } from 'chop-logic-components';

// Basic avatar
<Avatar
  name="Jane Doe"
  imageUrl="https://example.com/avatar.jpg"
/>

// Avatar with link
<Avatar
  name="Jane Doe"
  tooltip="jane.doe@example.com"
  imageUrl="https://example.com/avatar.jpg"
  link="https://example.com/profile/jane"
  linkTarget={LinkTarget.Blank}
/>
```

## Best Practices

- _Accessibility_: The component automatically uses the `name` prop as `alt` text on the image, ensuring screen readers can identify the person represented.
- _Fallback Behavior_: When the image URL is broken or unavailable, the component displays the uppercase first letter of the name in a styled circle. The fallback includes an `aria-label` for screen reader support.
- _External Links_: When `linkTarget` is set to `_blank`, the component adds `rel="noopener noreferrer"` for security and appends "(opens in new window)" to the `aria-label` for screen reader users.
- _Keyboard Navigation_: Linked avatars use a semantic `<a>` element, making them naturally focusable and navigable via keyboard.

## Props

<Controls />

atoms-button--docs.name: Docs

atoms-button--docs.content:
import { Meta, Controls } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";
import * as ButtonStories from "./Button.stories";

<Meta of={ButtonStories} title="Button" />

# Button

The `Button` component provides a flexible, accessible button with multiple visual styles and configurations. It supports text, icons, tooltips, and various interaction states.

## Usage

```tsx
import Button, { ButtonView, IconName } from "chop-logic-components";

const ExampleForm = () => (
  <form>
    <Button view={ButtonView.Primary} text="Submit" icon={IconName.Check} onClick={() => console.log("Clicked!")} />
    <Button view={ButtonView.Icon} icon={IconName.Settings} label="Settings" />
  </form>
);
```

## Best Practices

1. _Accessibility_: Always provide a label prop for icon-only buttons to ensure screen reader compatibility.
2. _Visual Hierarchy_: Use primary buttons for main actions and secondary buttons for less important actions.
3. _State Management_: Use the disabled state appropriately to prevent user confusion.
4. _Tooltips_: Add tooltips for buttons that need additional context, especially icon-only buttons.
5. _Icons_: Choose icons that are universally recognizable and match the button's purpose.

## Props

<Controls />

import { Meta, Controls } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks"; import * as HeaderStories from "./Header.stories"; # Header The `Header` component provides a flexible heading element that can render as any HTML heading level (h1-h6) and optionally include an icon. It's designed to maintain consistent styling across all heading levels while providing semantic HTML structure. ## Usage ```tsx import { Header, IconName } from "chop-logic-components"; const PageHeader = () => (
Welcome
Section Title
With Custom Class
); export default PageHeader; ``` ## Best Practices 1. _Semantic Hierarchy_: Use the appropriate heading level (`as` prop) to maintain proper document structure (h1 for main title, h2 for sections, h3 for subsections, etc.). 2. _Icon Selection_: Choose icons that are relevant to the header content and enhance understanding. 3. _Accessibility_: Ensure heading levels follow a logical hierarchy for screen reader users. 4. _Content Clarity_: Keep header text concise and descriptive. 5. _Consistent Styling_: Use the className prop for consistent styling across similar header levels. 6. _ID Usage_: Provide id attributes when headers need to be linked or referenced. ## Props

atoms-image--docs.name: Docs
atoms-image--docs.title: Atoms/Image
atoms-image--docs.content:
import { Meta, IconGallery, IconItem, Controls } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";
import * as ImageStories from "./Image.stories";
import { Icon } from "@components/atoms";
import { IconName } from "@enums";

<Meta of={ImageStories} title="Image" />

# Image

A responsive and accessible image component that supports multiple source sets, aspect ratio control, lazy loading, and comprehensive error handling for optimal performance and user experience.

## Usage

```tsx
import { Image } from './Image';

// Basic image with lazy loading
<Image
  src="https://example.com/image.jpg"
  alt="Descriptive alt text"
  width="400"
  height="300"
/>

// Image with caption and aspect ratio
<Image
  src="https://example.com/photo.jpg"
  alt="Beautiful landscape"
  caption="Sunset over the mountains"
  aspectRatio="16/9"
/>

// Responsive image with multiple sources
<Image
  src="https://example.com/large.jpg"
  alt="Responsive image example"
  sources={[
    {
      src: 'https://example.com/small.jpg',
      descriptor: '400w',
      media: '(max-width: 480px)',
    },
    {
      src: 'https://example.com/medium.jpg',
      descriptor: '800w',
      media: '(max-width: 1024px)',
    }
  ]}
  sizes="(max-width: 480px) 400px, (max-width: 1024px) 800px, 1200px"
/>
```

## Best Practices

1. _Accessibility_: Always provide meaningful alt text unless the image is purely decorative (use decorative=true and empty alt in that case)
2. _Performance_: Use loading="lazy" for below-the-fold images and decoding="async" for non-critical images
3. _Responsive Images_: Utilize the sources prop with appropriate media queries and descriptors for different screen sizes
4. _Aspect Ratios_: Use the aspectRatio prop to maintain consistent proportions and prevent layout shifts
5. _Error Handling_: Implement onError callbacks to handle broken images gracefully with fallback content
6. _Captions_: Use the caption prop to provide additional context when needed
7. _File Optimization_: Serve images in modern formats (WebP, AVIF) when possible and appropriate sizes for each breakpoint

## Props

<Controls />

); ``` ## Best Practices 1. _Association_: Always associate labels with form controls using htmlFor/inputId. Ensure inputId matches the ID of the associated control. 2. _Required Fields_: Use required prop to indicate mandatory fields. The asterisk (\*) is wrapped in `` with `title="required"`. 3. _Screen Readers_: Hidden text (when isTextHidden=true) remains accessible. Icons should have appropriate alt text or be decorative. 4. _Consistency_: Maintain consistent icon positioning throughout your application. Standardize required field indicators. 5. _Placement_: Position labels close to their associated controls. Consider vertical alignment for complex forms. ## Props

import { Meta, Controls } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";
import * as LinkStories from "./Link.stories";

<Meta of={LinkStories} title="Atoms/Link" />

# Link

The `Link` component provides an accessible, flexible link implementation with icon support and automatic external link handling.

## Usage

```tsx
import Link, { IconName } from "chop-logic-components";

const Navigation = () => (
  <nav>
    <Link href="/home">Home</Link>
    <Link href="https://external-site.com" icon={IconName.External} external>
      External Site
    </Link>
  </nav>
);
```

## Best Practices

- _Automatic External Link Detection_: Automatically detects external URLs and applies security attributes.
- _Icon Support_: Optional icons with configurable positioning.
- _Accessibility_: Proper ARIA labels for external links and screen reader support.
- _Flexible_: Supports all standard anchor element attributes.

## Props

<Controls />

atoms-portal--docs.title: Atoms/Portal
atoms-portal--docs.content:
import { Meta, Controls } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks"; import * as PortalStories from "./Portal.stories"; # Portal The `Portal` component is a helper container for rendering modals, tooltips, popups and other elements on top of the main page. The portal is created dynamically by appending a new `div` to the `document.body`. The component cleans up by removing the `div` from the DOM when it is unmounted. ### Usage ```tsx import Portal from "chop-logic-components"; const MyModalComponent = () => (
Hello from portal!
 ); export default MyModalComponent; ``` ## Best Practices 1. _Accessibility_: Ensure portal content is properly accessible via keyboard navigation and screen readers. 2. _Z-index Management_: Coordinate z-index values across portal content to maintain proper layering. 3. _Event Bubbling_: Remember that events from portal content bubble through the React tree, not the DOM tree. 4. _Container Selection_: Use specific container elements when you need precise control over portal placement. 5. _Cleanup_: Portal content is automatically cleaned up when the component unmounts. 6. _Use Cases_: Ideal for modals, tooltips, dropdowns, and any content that needs to escape overflow:hidden or z-index constraints. ## Props

atoms-tag--docs.content:
import { Meta, Controls } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";
import * as TagStories from "./Tag.stories";

<Meta of={TagStories} title="Tag" />

# Tag

The `Tag` component renders a keyword or category label as an inline pill. It supports an optional tooltip tooltip shown on hover and a custom background color via CSS custom properties.

## Usage

```tsx
import { Tag } from "chop-logic-components";

<Tag name="React" />

<Tag name="TypeScript" tooltip="A typed superset of JavaScript" />

<Tag name="Success" color="#4CAF50" />
```

## Best Practices

1. _Accessibility_: When a `tooltip` is provided, it is exposed via a Tooltip with `aria-describedby`, allowing screen readers to announce additional context when the tag receives focus.
2. _Color Usage_: Use the `color` prop sparingly for semantic meaning (e.g., category differentiation). The default theme color works well in most cases.
3. _Empty Names_: Avoid passing an empty `name` prop in production — the tag will render with no visible text, which may confuse users.
4. _Keyboard Navigation_: Tags are focusable via `tabIndex`. When focused, screen readers announce the tag label from its text content.

## Props

<Controls />

molecules-breadcrumbs--docs.name: Docs
molecules-breadcrumbs--docs.title: Molecules/Breadcrumbs
molecules-breadcrumbs--docs.content:
import { Meta, Controls } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";
import * as BreadcrumbsStories from "./Breadcrumbs.stories";

<Meta of={BreadcrumbsStories} title="Breadcrumbs" />

# Breadcrumbs

The `Breadcrumbs` component provides an accessible navigation aid that helps users understand their current location within a website hierarchy, following W3C accessibility guidelines.

## Usage

```tsx
import Breadcrumbs, { IconName } from "chop-logic-components";

const PageBreadcrumbs = () => (
  <Breadcrumbs
    items={[
      { label: "Home", icon: IconName.Home, link: "/" },
      { label: "Products", link: "/products" },
      { label: "Electronics", link: "/products/electronics" },
      { label: "Smartphones" }, // Current page - no link
    ]}
  />
);
```

## Best Practices

1. _Flexible Navigation_: Mix of linked and non-linked items
2. _Responsive Design_: Adapts to different screen sizes
3. _Accessibility_: Proper semantic structure and screen reader support
4. _Visual Separators_: Icon-based separators that are ignored by screen readers
5. _Icon Support_: Optional icons for enhanced visual context

## Props

molecules-numericinput--docs.name: Docs
molecules-numericinput--docs.title: Molecules/NumericInput
molecules-numericinput--docs.content:
import { Meta, Controls } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";
import * as Stories from "./NumericInput.stories";

<Meta of={Stories} title="NumericInput" />

# NumericInput

The `NumericInput` component is a flexible numeric input field that supports both stateful and stateless modes. It includes spin buttons, validation, and accessibility features.

## Usage

### Stateful (Default)

When `stateless` is false or omitted, the component manages its own state internally. Perfect for simple forms where you don't need external control.

```tsx
import NumericInput from "chop-logic-components";

<NumericInput name="quantity" label="Quantity" defaultValue={1} min={1} max={10} onChange={(event) => console.log(event)} />;
```

### Stateless

When `stateless` is true, the component is fully controlled via the `value` prop. You manage state externally and provide all handlers.

```tsx
import NumericInput from "chop-logic-components";

const [quantity, setQuantity] = useState(5);

<NumericInput
  name="product-qty"
  label="Quantity"
  stateless
  value={quantity}
  min={1}
  max={50}
  onChange={(event) => setQuantity(Number(event.target.value))}
  onIncrement={() => setQuantity((prev) => prev + 1)}
  onDecrement={() => setQuantity((prev) => prev - 1)}
/>;
```

## Best Practices

1. _Validation_:
   - Always provide errorMessage when using validation
   - Combine built-in min/max with custom validation when needed
   - Display validation errors clearly
2. _Value Ranges_:
   - Set sensible defaults for min/max
   - Consider the expected range of values
   - Adjust step size appropriately for the context
3. _Accessibility Notes_:
   - Use label prop for clear identification
   - Ensure spin buttons are keyboard accessible
   - Provide meaningful error messages for screen readers

## Props

<Controls />

molecules-textinput--docs.name: Docs
molecules-textinput--docs.title: Molecules/TextInput
molecules-textinput--docs.content:
import { Meta, Controls } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";
import * as TextInput from "./TextInput.stories";

<Meta of={TextInput} title="TextInput" />

# TextInput

The `TextInput` component is a flexible text input field that supports both stateful and stateless modes. It includes validation, clear button, and password visibility toggle features.

## Usage

### Stateful (Default)

When `stateless` is false or omitted, the component manages its own state internally. Perfect for simple forms where you don't need external control.

```tsx
import TextInput from "chop-logic-components";

<TextInput name="email" label="Email address" type="email" placeholder="Enter your email" required onChange={(event) => console.log(event)} />;
```

### Stateless

When `stateless` is true, the component is fully controlled via the `value` prop. You manage state externally and provide all handlers.

```tsx
import TextInput from "chop-logic-components";

const [message, setMessage] = useState("");

<TextInput name="message" label="Your Message" stateless value={message} placeholder="Type your message..." onChange={(event) => setMessage(event.target.value)} onClear={() => setMessage("")} />;
```

## Best Practices

1. _Labeling_:
   - Always provide a meaningful label
   - Use placeholder text sparingly (not as a label replacement)
   - Ensure proper label-input association
2. _Validation_:
   - Provide clear error messages
   - Validate on blur as well as submit
   - Consider real-time validation for long forms
3. _Accessibility_:
   - Ensure clear focus states for all interactive elements
   - Ensure Clear and Password Toggle buttons are keyboard accessible
   - Use ARIA attributes for dynamic content updates
4. _Controlled vs Uncontrolled_:
   - Use stateful mode (default) for simple, isolated inputs
   - Use stateless mode when you need fine-grained control or integration with state management

## Props

organisms-dialog--docs.name: Docs
organisms-dialog--docs.title: Organisms/Dialog
organisms-dialog--docs.content:
import { Meta, Controls } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks"; import * as Dialog from "./Dialog.stories"; # Dialog The `Dialog` allows you to render a responsive pop-up window with any content. The dialog appearance trigger can be a button or any event on the page. If there are several dialogs, they will appear on top of each other in the order they are called. When adding a dialog, you must think through the conditions for opening and closing it, so props `isOpened` and `onClose` are mandatory. ## Usage ```tsx import Dialog from "chop-logic-components"; const ConfirmationDialog = ({ isOpen, onClose }) => { return (

Are you sure you want to perform this action?

 ); }; ``` ## Best Practices 1. _Content Structure_: - Keep titles concise - Organize content logically - Place primary actions on the right 2. _User Experience_: - Avoid overwhelming users with too much information - Use dialogs for critical interactions - Provide clear exit options 3. _Performance_: - Load heavy content dynamically - Consider virtualization for long content - Memoize dialog contents when appropriate 4. _Accessibility_: - Focus is trapped within the dialog when open - Escape key closes dialog - Announces role="dialog" and aria-modal="true" - Test with screen readers - Ensure keyboard-only navigation - Use ARIA attributes appropriately ## Props

organisms-menu--docs.name: Docs
organisms-menu--docs.title: Organisms/Menu
organisms-menu--docs.content:
import { Meta, Controls } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";
import * as MenuStories from "./Menu.stories";

<Meta of={MenuStories} title="Menu" />

# Menu

The `Menu` component provides an accessible navigation menu with support for both vertical and horizontal orientations, nested items, and flexible interaction modes.

## Usage

```tsx
import Menu, { OrientationMode } from "chop-logic-components";

const AppMenu = () => {
  const menuItems = [
    {
      id: "home",
      label: "Home",
      link: "/home",
      icon: IconName.Home,
    },
    {
      id: "products",
      label: "Products",
      nestedItems: [{ id: "product-1", label: "Product 1", link: "/products/1" }],
    },
  ];

  return <Menu items={menuItems} mode={OrientationMode.Horizontal} openedOn="hover" />;
};
```

## Best Practices

1. _Organization_:
   - Limit nesting depth (recommend max 2-3 levels)
   - Group related items together
   - Sort items logically
2. _Interaction_:
   - Use hover activation for quick access
   - Use click activation for complex menus
   - Provide visual feedback for active items
3. _Performance_:
   - Memoize menu items when possible
   - Consider dynamic loading for large menus
4. _Keyboard Navigation_:
   - Tab to navigate through menu items
   - Enter/Space to activate items
   - Arrow keys to navigate nested menus
   - Escape to close submenus

## Props

<Controls />

organisms-previewcard--docs.name: Docs
organisms-previewcard--docs.title: Organisms/PreviewCard
organisms-previewcard--docs.content:
import { Meta, Controls } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";
import * as PreviewCardStories from "./PreviewCard.stories";

<Meta of={PreviewCardStories} title="PreviewCard" />

# PreviewCard

The `PreviewCard` is an organism component that displays an article or external resource preview in a card layout. It composes the `Avatar` and `Tag` atom components along with the existing `Image` atom to present a rich content summary including an image, title, summary text, a list of authors, and keyword tags.

## Usage

```tsx
import { PreviewCard } from "chop-logic-components";

const ArticlePreview = () => {
  return (
    <PreviewCard
      link="https://example.com/article"
      image={{ src: "/images/article.jpg", alt: "Article cover" }}
      title="Building Accessible Component Libraries"
      summary="A guide to creating reusable, accessible React components."
      authors={[
        { id: "1", name: "Jane Smith", email: "jane@example.com", imageUrl: "/avatars/jane.jpg", link: "https://example.com/jane" },
        { id: "2", name: "John Doe", email: "john@example.com", imageUrl: "/avatars/john.jpg" },
      ]}
      tags={[
        { id: "1", name: "React", color: "#61DAFB", tooltip: "A JavaScript library for building UIs" },
        { id: "2", name: "TypeScript", color: "#3178C6" },
      ]}
    />
  );
};
```

## Composition

PreviewCard composes the following components:

- **Image** (atom) — renders the card header image with responsive behavior and fallback
- **Avatar** (atom) — renders each author as a circular image with optional link and fallback letter
- **Tag** (atom) — renders each tag as an inline label with optional tooltip and custom color

The card renders these sections in order: image link, title (`h3`), optional summary (`p`), optional authors list (`ul`), and optional tags list (`ul`). When `authors` or `tags` arrays are empty, their respective sections are omitted entirely.

## Best Practices

1. _Content_:
   - Provide a meaningful `title` for both visual display and accessibility
   - Use descriptive `alt` text for the card image
   - Keep `summary` concise — it serves as a teaser
2. _Links_:
   - Use `linkTarget="_blank"` for external resources
   - The component automatically adds `rel="noopener noreferrer"` for blank targets
   - Each author can have an independent link with its own target
3. _Accessibility_:
   - The root `<article>` element has an `aria-label` derived from the title
   - The image link includes an `aria-label` "View article: title"
   - External links announce "(opens in new window)" to screen readers
   - Authors and tags sections use semantic `<ul>` lists with `aria-label`
   - Test with keyboard navigation and screen readers
4. _Theming_:
   - All colors use CSS variables and respond to light/dark theme automatically
   - Tags accept custom `color` for visual categorization

## Props

<Controls />

organisms-tabs--docs.name: Docs
organisms-tabs--docs.title: Organisms/Tabs
organisms-tabs--docs.content:
import { Meta, Controls } from "@storybook/addon-docs/blocks";
import * as TabsStories from "./Tabs.stories";

<Meta of={TabsStories} title="Tabs" />

# Tabs

The `Tabs` component provides an accessible tabbed interface with support for both horizontal and vertical orientations, disabled states, editable titles, and dynamic tab management.

## Usage

```tsx
import Tabs, { OrientationMode } from "chop-logic-components";

const ProductTabs = () => {
  const tabs = [
    {
      id: "details",
      title: "Details",
      content: <ProductDetails />,
    },
    {
      id: "specs",
      title: "Specifications",
      content: <ProductSpecs />,
    },
  ];

  return <Tabs tabs={tabs} mode={OrientationMode.Horizontal} defaultTabId="specs" />;
};
```

### Best Practices

1. _Organization_:
   - Keep tab titles concise (1-2 words)
   - Group related content together
   - Limit number of tabs (recommend max 5-7)
2. _Content_:
   - Load heavy content dynamically
   - Consider lazy-loading hidden tabs
   - Maintain consistent tab heights
3. _States_:
   - Clearly indicate active tab
   - Visually distinguish disabled tabs
   - Provide loading states for async content
4. _Dynamic Tab Management_:
   - Provide meaningful default names for new tabs
   - Consider implementing tab reordering if needed
   - Validate tab titles to prevent empty or duplicate names
   - Provide undo functionality for accidental tab deletion

## Props

<Controls />
